reasoning: The prediction market priced this event at 0.35 on March 6, when Bitcoin was trading around $69,880. As of mid-March (March 14-15), BTC is trading around $70,800, having briefly hit a local high near $74,000 on March 13 but failing to sustain that momentum. Since Bitcoin has not yet touched $80,000 in March, the resolution depends entirely on the remaining 16 days of the month. While Bitcoin is slightly closer in absolute price to the $80,000 target than it was on March 6 (needing roughly a 13% move now as opposed to a 14.5% move then), the time remaining has dropped significantly from 26 days to 16 days. Since time decay has accelerated but the price has not advanced enough to offset this, the required volatility has increased and the probability has logically decreased. An options market quote from March 11 also implies only a 35% probability of expiring above $80K by June, which is consistent with a lower probability of reaching it in March. A drop from 0.35 to 0.23 accurately reflects the time decay.
